description: A square, high-contrast black-and-white designed arrest card from ICE. The background is a tightly cropped grayscale photograph of a pair of handcuffed hands wrapped in chains, conveying captivity. Overlaid typography in the upper left, in pale cyan/teal all-caps sans-serif, reads 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL', with a smaller white subhead '1/26/2025 | Dallas, Texas'. Below, in large bold white type, the subject's name is stacked across four lines: 'Jeering Alcides Godoy Linares'. Beneath that a bold white label 'CRIMES:' is followed by a bulleted list: 'Assault' and 'Evading arrest'. The U.S. Department of Health [sic] Homeland Security circular seal and the white 'ICE' wordmark appear at the bottom left. A red diagonal banner in the lower-right corner reads 'DALLAS' in white, branding the field office. The composition uses ominous monochrome imagery, a wanted-poster/rogues-gallery layout, and prominent listing of alleged crimes to portray the arrestee as a dangerous criminal and to publicize ICE enforcement. Target audience is the public/supporters of immigration enforcement.
notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 1/26/2025 | Dallas, Texas; Jeering Alcides Godoy Linares; CRIMES: Assault, Evading arrest; DALLAS; ICE
